settings.title: La Maison
settings.tagline: French-inspired cuisine in the heart of the city

children.text: La Maison is a French-inspired restaurant offering seasonal dishes crafted with the finest local ingredients. Join us for an unforgettable dining experience.

data.name: French Onion Soup
data.description: Slow-cooked caramelized onions in a rich beef broth, topped with a crusty crouton and melted Gruyere cheese.

$media.alt: French onion soup with melted cheese

data.name: Tuna Tartare
data.description: Fresh ahi tuna with avocado, sesame, ginger, and a citrus soy dressing. Served with crispy wonton chips.

data.name: Escargots de Bourgogne
data.description: Classic Burgundy snails baked in garlic-parsley butter, served with crusty bread for dipping.

data.name: Duck Confit
data.description: Slow-cooked duck leg, crispy skin, served with lentils du Puy, roasted garlic, and a red wine jus.

$media.alt: Duck confit with crispy golden skin

data.name: Filet Mignon au Poivre
data.description: 8oz center-cut filet with cracked peppercorn crust, cognac cream sauce, and pommes puree.

data.name: Bouillabaisse
data.description: Traditional Provencal seafood stew with mussels, shrimp, white fish, and saffron-tomato broth. Served with rouille and grilled bread.

data.name: Coq au Vin
data.description: Braised chicken in Burgundy wine with pearl onions, mushrooms, and lardons. A timeless French classic.

$media.alt: Coq au vin braised chicken dish

data.name: Pan-Seared Salmon
data.description: Atlantic salmon with crispy skin, beurre blanc, asparagus, and fingerling potatoes.

data.name: Creme Brulee
data.description: Classic vanilla bean custard with a caramelized sugar crust. Simple, elegant, perfect.

data.name: Chocolate Fondant
data.description: Warm dark chocolate cake with a molten center, served with vanilla ice cream and gold leaf.

data.name: Tarte Tatin
data.description: Upside-down caramelized apple tart with flaky puff pastry and Chantilly cream.

data.name: French 75
data.description: Gin, fresh lemon juice, simple syrup, topped with champagne. Effervescent and timeless.

data.name: Bordeaux Rouge
data.description: A curated selection from Saint-Emilion. Rich, full-bodied, with notes of dark cherry and cedar.

data.name: Espresso Martini
data.description: Vodka, fresh espresso, coffee liqueur, and vanilla. A bold ending or a spirited beginning.

data.title: The Dining Room

$media.alt: Elegant restaurant dining room with warm lighting

data.description: Our main dining room seats 60 guests in an atmosphere of warmth and sophistication.

data.title: The Kitchen

$media.alt: Chef at work in a professional kitchen

data.description: Where the magic happens. Our open kitchen lets you watch the culinary artistry unfold.

data.title: The Art of Plating
data.description: Every dish is a canvas. Our chefs take pride in presentation as much as flavor.

data.title: The Wine Cellar

$media.alt: Wine bottles in a rustic cellar

data.description: Over 200 labels from France and beyond. Our sommelier will guide your pairing.

data.title: The Terrace

$media.alt: Outdoor restaurant terrace with string lights

data.description: Al fresco dining under the stars. Available seasonally from May through October.

data.title: Sweet Finales

$media.alt: Elegant dessert with berries and chocolate

data.description: Our pastry chef creates desserts that are as stunning to look at as they are to taste.

data.title: The Bar

$media.alt: Stylish cocktail bar with ambient lighting

data.description: Craft cocktails and aperitifs. The perfect place to begin or end your evening.

data.title: Farm to Table

$media.alt: Fresh vegetables and herbs from local farms

data.description: We source directly from local farms and markets to ensure the freshest ingredients.

data.quote: An extraordinary dining experience. The duck confit was the best I've had outside of Paris. The ambiance, the service, every detail was perfect.
We celebrated our anniversary here and it was unforgettable. The tasting menu was a journey through French cuisine at its finest. Highly recommended.
The attention to detail is remarkable. From the amuse-bouche to the petit fours, every course showed real passion and craftsmanship. A gem in the city.
La Maison has become our go-to for special occasions. The wine selection is outstanding and the sommelier's recommendations are always spot-on.

children.text: La Maison was born from a simple dream: to bring the warmth, elegance, and soul of French countryside dining to the heart of the city. Since opening our doors in 2010, we have been committed to creating memorable experiences through food, atmosphere, and genuine hospitality.
Our Chef

Chef Antoine Duval trained in some of the finest kitchens across Lyon and Paris before bringing his vision to La Maison. His philosophy is rooted in respect for ingredients, classical technique, and a touch of modern creativity. Every dish on our menu reflects his dedication to the craft and his deep love for French culinary tradition.
Our Ingredients

We believe great food starts at the source. We work directly with local farms, fisheries, and artisan producers to ensure the freshest seasonal ingredients make it to your plate. Our menu changes with the seasons, reflecting what's at its peak and what inspires our kitchen team.
The Space

Our dining room blends rustic charm with modern sophistication. Exposed brick, warm lighting, and carefully curated details create an atmosphere that feels both intimate and celebratory. Whether you're joining us for a quiet dinner for two or a lively gathering with friends, La Maison is designed to make every occasion feel special.
Cooking is an act of love, a gift, a way of sharing with others the little secrets that are whispered to you by the stoves of the world.
Private Events

Our private dining room accommodates up to 24 guests, perfect for celebrations, corporate dinners, and intimate gatherings. We offer customized menus and wine pairings tailored to your event. Contact us to discuss your next special occasion.

We warmly welcome reservations to ensure the best experience for all our guests. While walk-ins are always welcome based on availability, we recommend booking in advance, especially for weekend evenings and special occasions.
How to Reserve
You can make a reservation by calling us at +1 (555) 123-4567, sending an email to reservations@lamaison.com, or using the reservation form on this page. We will confirm your booking within 24 hours.
Cancellation Policy
We kindly ask that you notify us at least 24 hours in advance if you need to cancel or modify your reservation. This allows us to accommodate other guests who may be waiting for a table.
Large Parties
For groups of 8 or more, please contact us directly by phone so we can arrange the perfect setting for your gathering. Our private dining room is available for parties of up to 24 guests.
Dietary Requirements

Please let us know about any dietary restrictions or allergies when making your reservation. Our kitchen is happy to accommodate vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and other dietary needs with advance notice.
